methodology: Each entity status is a filed claim with a public source. The seed file is hand-curated from the EFTA corpus, the public knowledge graph at epstein-data.com, and the SDNY dockets. Future scrapes (CourtListener, DOJ press, Google News RSS) update these statuses. Submit a source URL to update an entry.

perpetrators.name: Jeffrey Epstein
perpetrators.summary: Found dead August 10, 2019 in Manhattan Correctional Center. Autopsy: suicide by hanging. Two private pathologists (Dr. Michael Baden, Dr. Mark Haronis) hired by brother Mark Epstein ruled the death a homicide by strangulation. FBI and DOJ OIG reports on file.

perpetrators.name: Ghislaine Maxwell
perpetrators.summary: Convicted December 29, 2021 on 5 federal counts (sex trafficking of minors, transporting minors to engage in illegal sex acts). 20-year sentence. Serving at Tallahassee FCI. No cooperation agreement.

perpetrators.name: Jean-Luc Brunel
perpetrators.summary: Found dead March 19, 2022 in his Paris apartment. Suicide by hanging, same night of his scheduled testimony in the Giammati case. French prosecutors opened an investigation into the circumstances of his death.

associates.name: Black
associates.summary: Apollo Global Management co-founder. Paid Epstein $158M-$1.08B (per different filings, 2012-2017) for "tax and estate planning" services. Stepped down as Apollo CEO March 2021, after internal review. No criminal charges. Settled with Virgin Islands AG 2023 ($120M+ commitment to Virgin Islands).
